series.title: [PATCH 0/2] Replace assert with bound checks in PnvPHB3

review.summary: Patch 2 introduces a build-breaking error in `pci_host_config_read_common()`: the new debug path references `val`, but that variable does not exist in the read helper. The series should not be merged until that is fixed.

stages.A: Patch intent is sound: patch 1 prevents PHB3 from issuing invalid >4-byte config accesses, and patch 2 aims to harden the generic PCI host helpers against invalid lengths.
stages.B: Static review found a compile-time failure in patch 2. `pci_host_config_read_common()` logs `val` in the new `len > 4` path, but the function has no such local or parameter.
stages.C: Patch 1 behavior looks reasonable: invalid PHB3 config accesses are rejected early, with writes ignored and reads returning all-ones.
stages.D: The provided testing does not cover a full build, and `BUILD STATUS: configure_ok` would not catch the compile error introduced in patch 2.
stages.E: Overall assessment: reject as-is. After fixing the read-side debug statement, the hardening approach appears directionally correct.

findings.title: Undefined `val` in read-side invalid-length path breaks the build
findings.description: The new `len > 4` branch in `pci_host_config_read_common()` passes `val` to `PCI_DPRINTF`, but the read helper does not define a `val` variable. This causes a compile failure, so the series is not mergeable in its current form.
patch_context: +        PCI_DPRINTF("%s: invalid length access: addr " HWADDR_FMT_plx " \\
findings.suggestion: Remove `val` from the read-side debug message, or replace it with data that actually exists in this function. For example, log only `addr` and `len` before returning `~0x0`.
findings.confidence_reason: The function shown in the patch is the read helper, and the added call site explicitly references `val` despite no such symbol being present in that scope.

cover_letter:
Subject: [PATCH 0/2] Replace assert with bound checks in PnvPHB3
From: Aditya Gupta <adityag@linux.ibm.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Mar 2026 00:34:36 +0530
Message-Id: <20260326190438.734239-1-adityag@linux.ibm.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

Overview
========

PHB in Power8 supports 8 byte registers, and hence the ops structure
allows accessing of 8 bytes in 'pnv_phb3_reg_ops'

Both 'pnv_phb3_reg_read' & 'pnv_phb3_reg_write' pass the arguments as is
to 'pnv_phb3_config_{read,write}', if offset is PHB_CONFIG_DATA.

This when called with size as 8, causes following assert failure in
'pci_host_config_read_common' & 'pci_host_config_write_common':

    assert(len <= 4);

This patch series splits it into two patches:
1. Fix PnvPHB3 code so that it never calls pci_host_config_{read,write}_common
   with read/write size of >4, thus fixing the assert problem
2. Replace the assert with bounds checking

Patch #2 should not have any functional change, since the issue is
already fixed by patch #1

Testing
=======

1. Git repo with the patches: https://gitlab.com/adi-g15-ibm/qemu/tree/fix_assert_phb3

2. Gitlab pipeline: https://gitlab.com/adi-g15-ibm/qemu/-/pipelines/2410981612

> Note: One test is failing in the pipeline, but the log shows some cargo error,
> unrelated to the patch series

3. Testcase provided by Zexiang Zhang, this will fail without the patches:

  cat << "EOF" | ./qemu-system-ppc64 \
    -M powernv8 -accel tcg -cpu POWER8 \
    -display none \
    -serial none -monitor none \
    -qtest stdio -S \
    -d guest_errors -D /tmp/pci_len_pnv.log
  writeq 0x3fc009013c000 0x140
  writeq 0x3fc009013c010 0x8000000000000000
  writeq 0x3fc009013c000 0x130
  readq 0x3fc009013c010
  EOF

Aditya Gupta (2):
  ppc/pnv_phb3: Error out on invalid config access
  hw/pci: Replace assert with bounds check and return

 hw/pci-host/pnv_phb3.c | 10 ++++++++++
 hw/pci/pci_host.c      | 14 ++++++++++++--
 2 files changed, 22 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

-- 
2.53.0
